task: Ralph (2026-05-16 evening, R19): Two-part move-and-document. (1) git mv 5 internal PRD/planning dirs (SandboxAgnosticSandboxingPRD, devframeworkPRD, secureIntegrationsPRD, skillsPRD, servertodo) from the public doable repo to ../doablechore/ (already exists as internal-cruft dump). (2) Create a new PlatformTemplatesPRD/ in the doable repo with one detailed markdown file per supported deployment platform — full env-var contracts, deploy steps, gotchas. Full-stack platforms only (no Vercel/Netlify split). Skip mid-stage doctest deltas, focus on landing the full PRD in one batch.

stories.title: Move 5 internal PRD/planning dirs from doable/ to ../doablechore/
acceptanceCriteria: git mv runs cleanly for SandboxAgnosticSandboxingPRD, devframeworkPRD, secureIntegrationsPRD, skillsPRD, servertodo — destination = ../doablechore/<name>/
Repo-root `ls` no longer lists any of those 5 dirs
../doablechore/ contains all 5 dirs with their full contents preserved
git status shows the moves as renames (not delete + add) so history is preserved
Grep across services/ apps/ packages/ scripts/ docker/ — confirm no source file references any of the moved paths (no broken imports, no broken doc links)

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/_INDEX.md — top-level overview + scope decisions
acceptanceCriteria: PlatformTemplatesPRD/_INDEX.md exists with: (a) one-paragraph scope statement (full-stack platforms only, no split frontend hosting), (b) table of contents linking the 9 per-platform docs + 1 baseline doc + 1 release-pipeline doc, (c) explicit list of platforms we DO NOT support and why

Doc references the canonical env contract (DOABLE_KEK, JWT_SECRET, ENCRYPTION_KEY, INTERNAL_SECRET, INSTALL_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN, all 19 AI provider env vars, OAuth + Stripe optionals) and points readers at 00-baseline.md for the source of truth

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/00-baseline.md — shared env contract + image references
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the FULL env-var contract: required secrets (JWT_SECRET, ENCRYPTION_KEY, INTERNAL_SECRET, DOABLE_KEK, POSTGRES_PASSWORD) + how to generate each + bootstrap (INSTALL_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN/EXPIRES_AT) + AI seeds (all 19 with their seedAiProviderFromEnv.ts baseUrls) + OAuth/Stripe optionals
Documents the 4 ghcr.io image names + tag scheme (ghcr.io/doable-me/doable-{api,ws,web,migrate}:{vX.Y.Z,latest})
Documents the NEXT_PUBLIC_* runtime-placeholder mechanism (__DOABLE_API_URL__ → sed at startup) so per-platform docs can reference it without re-explaining
Documents the postgres + migrate + api/ws/web start ordering (migrate must run to completion before api/ws; web depends on api + ws being up)
States our network security invariant (services bind 127.0.0.1 only; one fronting proxy/ingress takes 80/443)

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/01-coolify.md — Coolify self-hosted PaaS
acceptanceCriteria: Documents Coolify install path: point Coolify's Docker Compose source at docker/docker-compose.prod.yml (pull from ghcr.io)
Documents Coolify-specific tweaks: env-secrets-via-UI mapping, persistent volume mounts, magic env handling for NEXT_PUBLIC_*
Documents the labels Coolify expects (if any) and whether we need a coolify.json manifest at all

Documents nginx/Traefik handling — Coolify provides its own reverse proxy, so our docker/setup.sh nginx is skipped; documents how Coolify exposes only ports 3000/4000/4001 internally and serves them at user-configured subdomains
Verification: 'as documented, an admin clicking through Coolify UI can reach the wizard at /setup without editing any compose YAML'

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/02-digitalocean.md — DO App Platform
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the exact changes needed to .do/app.yaml to make it deploy successfully: add DOABLE_KEK, all 19 AI provider env vars (as SECRET-scoped envs), INSTALL_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN, point service definitions at the ghcr.io images via image field (instead of dockerfile_path build path), add a migrate Job (DO supports pre-deploy jobs)

Documents the DO managed PostgreSQL wiring (DATABASE_URL=${doable-db.DATABASE_URL}) + pgvector caveat (DO doesn't ship pgvector — operator must enable extensions; document the SQL to run, or point at the alternative of running our pgvector image as a worker)
Documents the ports/routes mapping (web → /, api → /api, ws → /ws)
Documents the instance sizes recommended (basic-xs for api/ws, basic-xxs for web) and what fails on smaller plans (Next.js build OOMs)
Provides a 1-click 'Deploy to DO' button snippet + the matching app-spec validate command (`doctl apps spec validate .do/app.yaml`)

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/03-railway.md — Railway
acceptanceCriteria: Documents Railway service-per-process model: 4 separate Railway services (api, ws, web, migrate-as-startupHook) sharing one Postgres plugin
Documents exact railway.json template content + which fields are required (build, deploy, healthcheckPath)
Documents the Railway 'Deploy from Image' option pointing at ghcr.io/doable-me/doable-<service>:latest (avoids Railway's 8GB build OOM on Next.js)
Documents env-var setup: which can be shared via Railway 'shared variables' (POSTGRES_PASSWORD, JWT_SECRET, INTERNAL_SECRET, DOABLE_KEK) vs which must be per-service (NEXT_PUBLIC_* for web)
Documents migration handling: Railway's lack of native pre-deploy hooks → use the migrate image as a one-shot 'startup migration' service or set up a release command
Provides a 'Deploy on Railway' button template URL

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/04-render.md — Render
acceptanceCriteria: Documents Render's blueprint mechanism (render.yaml in repo root, auto-detected on connect)
Documents exact render.yaml template content: 3 web services (api/ws/web) + 1 cron/preDeployCommand for migrate + 1 managed Postgres
Documents Render's pgvector support (managed Postgres supports the extension; document the CREATE EXTENSION command and where to run it)
Documents image: ghcr.io/... usage so users can skip Render's source build
Documents the 'Deploy to Render' button URL pattern + the autoDeploy: false / true tradeoff

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/05-fly.md — Fly.io
acceptanceCriteria: Documents Fly's one-app-per-toml model: 3 separate apps (doable-api, doable-ws, doable-web) + 1 fly postgres cluster
Documents what goes in each fly.toml (build.image = ghcr.io/...) — three example tomls included
Documents Fly's internal DNS (.flycast / .internal) so api/ws/web can reach each other via doable-api.flycast etc., not via Docker compose network
Documents migration handling: `fly machine run` for one-shot migrate, OR a release_command in fly.toml
Documents Fly volumes for api_projects, api_thumbnails, ws_projects (per-region attached volumes)
Documents internal-only vs public services: api/ws should be internal (flycast), web should be public (with .fly.dev domain or custom)

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/06-kubernetes.md — Kubernetes (Kustomize + Helm path)
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the K8s resources needed: 4 Deployments (api/ws/web/migrate-as-Job), 4 Services (api/ws/web/postgres ClusterIP), 1 StatefulSet for postgres (or external managed DB), 1 Ingress (default: nginx-ingress with cert-manager for TLS), 1 Secret per credential category, 1 ConfigMap per non-secret env block, PVCs for api_projects/api_thumbnails/ws_projects
Documents Kustomize base/overlays structure (k8s/base/ + k8s/overlays/{dev,prod}/) so operators can layer environment-specific config
Documents the Job spec for migrate (runs once on apply, blocks api/ws from starting via initContainer or a Helm hook)
Documents the readiness/liveness probes for each service (api: /health, web: /, ws: TCP)
Documents the secret-management options (in-cluster Secret, External Secrets Operator, sealed-secrets) and recommends one default
Documents the alternative Helm chart approach (charts/doable/) and which path we ship FIRST (recommend: Kustomize first, Helm later — fewer files, simpler templating)

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/07-app-json.md — Heroku-style app.json for 1-click buttons
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the role of app.json: it's the schema Heroku/Render/Railway/some-others auto-detect to populate their 'Deploy this app' button

Documents the exact app.json content: name, description, repository, success_url, all required env vars with descriptions + value templates + secret generators ({{generate jwt-secret}} pattern), buildpacks (or pointer to Dockerfile), addons (heroku-postgresql)
Documents how this overlaps with .do/app.yaml, railway.json, render.yaml (it doesn't conflict — app.json is the meta-deploy-button schema; each platform also reads its own native file)
Documents the README badge URLs the file enables: Deploy to Heroku, Deploy to Render, Deploy to Railway

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/08-codespaces.md — GitHub Codespaces / VS Code Dev Containers
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the .devcontainer/devcontainer.json file we'll ship: Node 22 + pnpm + Postgres + Docker-in-Docker features, ports 3000/4000/4001/5432 forwarded, postCreateCommand running pnpm install + setup-server.sh in localhost mode
Documents the included VS Code extensions list (ESLint, Prettier, Tailwind, GitLens, etc.) — keep it tight
Documents the recommended Codespaces machine size (4-core/16GB minimum because of the Next.js build)
Documents how the 'Open in Codespaces' README badge works + the exact URL pattern
Documents what's DIFFERENT from local dev: the Codespaces forwarded-port URLs (which are HTTPS by default with a synthetic cert) and how that affects NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL config

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/09-release-pipeline.md — ghcr.io publish workflow + versioning policy
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the .github/workflows/publish-docker-images.yml workflow's exact triggers (push of `v*.*.*` tag + manual workflow_dispatch) and matrix structure (api/ws/web/migrate)
Documents the versioning policy: semver, `latest` always tracks the most recent vX.Y.Z, never built off main HEAD between releases
Documents the post-publish checklist: make package public on ghcr.io (manual step in GitHub UI), update README badges, smoke-test the published image with docker/setup.sh --prebuilt
Documents the per-platform release impact: docs in this PRD reference :latest by default, but recommend pinning :vX.Y.Z in prod templates

stories.title: Create PlatformTemplatesPRD/10-repo-cleanup.md — public-release cleanup checklist
acceptanceCriteria: Documents the .dockerignore audit findings and the specific lines that must be added (PRD planning dirs in chore now, scripts/r*-*.ts, CLAUDE.md, prd.json, progress.txt, etc.)
Documents the git remote cleanup (drop the codehub remote before publishing, OR keep as a private mirror)
Documents the secret-scan approach (grep for sk-, sk-ant-, sk-cp-, eyJ JWTs across the repo; document what to do if anything is found)
Documents the recommended pre-tag-release ritual: run the cleanup checklist, push, then `git tag v0.1.0` to trigger the publish workflow
